index.html.title: FooDoc
index.html.name: FooDoc
index.html.summary: A Bootstrap and Handlebars based template for JSDoc3.

base_chains.html.title: base/chains
base_chains.html.name: base/chains
base_chains.html.description: Chains define the primary composition elements (functions) that determine the order of execution.

base_chains.html#close.title: close
base_chains.html#close.name: close
base_chains.html#close.description: The close method asks an object to shut itself down in a way that will allow it to be reopened, unlike the end method which will call the destroy method which should make the object unusable, but also devoid of all resources whereas close may still keep some resources open. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Bar Food This is a table This uses the before chain which means the last one defined in the first one destroyed

base_chains.html#end.title: end
base_chains.html#end.name: end
base_chains.html#end.description: The end method will call the destroy method which should make the object unusable and devoid of all resources, unlike the close method asks an object to shut itself down in a way that will allow it to be reopened. This uses the before chain which means the last one defined in the first one destroyed

base_chains.html#destroy.title: <private> destroy
base_chains.html#destroy.name: destroy
base_chains.html#destroy.description: Destroy is called by the end method and it is here that you should clean up after yourself. The difference between destroy and end is the end is the verb that you raise on an object to ask it to go away and destroy is where you actually do the work to clean up. Think of this as the counterpart of constructor yet not called automatically. This uses the before chain which means the last one defined is the first one destroyed

base_chains.html#open.title: open
base_chains.html#open.name: open
base_chains.html#open.description: If you are using the open/close paradigm for an object that can kind of go dormant on base/chains#close and can be "reopened" again later, here is where the "open" code will go. This used the after chain which means that the first one defined is the first one destroyed.

utils_logger.Logger.html.title: Logger
utils_logger.Logger.html.name: Logger
utils_logger.Logger.html.description: A logger class that you can mix into your classes to handle logging settings and state at an object level. See utils/logger for the members of this class

utils_logger.Logger.html#silent.title: silent()
utils_logger.Logger.html#silent.name: silent
utils_logger.Logger.html#silent.description: Turn off all logging. If you log something, it will not error, but will not do anything either and the cycles are minimal.

utils_logger.Logger.html#level.title: level( lvl )
utils_logger.Logger.html#level.name: level
utils_logger.Logger.html#level.description: Sets the logging level to one of trace, debug, info, warn, error.

base.html.title: base
base.html.name: base
base.html.description: The base of all classes in the system, this is one of the few pure "classes" in core the of the system. It is a pretty clean little class whose primary purpose is to surface the composition chains and a basis for storing options on mixin and subclass instances. Options are handled at the instance rather than the prototype level so that multiple instances don't compete for default values.

base.html#close.title: close
base.html#close.name: close
base.html#close.description: The close method asks an object to shut itself down in a way that will allow it to be reopened, unlike the end method which will call the destroy method which should make the object unusable, but also devoid of all resources whereas close may still keep some resources open. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Bar Food This is a table This uses the before chain which means the last one defined in the first one destroyed

base.html#open.title: open
base.html#open.name: open
base.html#open.description: If you are using the open/close paradigm for an object that can kind of go dormant on base/chains#close and can be "reopened" again later, here is where the "open" code will go. This used the after chain which means that the first one defined is the first one destroyed.

base.html#addOptions.title: addOptions( options )
base.html#addOptions.name: addOptions
base.html#addOptions.description: Add an option to a class. If any members of the hash already exist in this.options, they will be overwritten.

base.html#addDefaultOptions.title: addDefaultOptions( options )
base.html#addDefaultOptions.name: addDefaultOptions
base.html#addDefaultOptions.description: Add a default option to a class. The default options are only set if there is not already a value for the option.

base.html#end.title: end()
base.html#end.name: end
base.html#end.description: Call this to close your object and dispose of all maintained resources. You can define this method on your own classes without having to call the superclass instance, however it is reccomended that you put all disposal code in destroy(). You must be disciplined about calling this on your instances.

base.html#destroy.title: destroy()
base.html#destroy.name: destroy
base.html#destroy.description: Called when it is time to get rid of all of your instance level references and objects and events. You can define this method on your own classes without having to call the superclass instance. It is called by instance.end() automatically

documents_model.html.title: documents/model
documents_model.html.name: documents/model
documents_model.html.description: A model is the first level if usable data-bearing entity in the system. It does NOT include any verbs for saving or anything like that, it is a pure, in memory data container

documents_model.html#_idField.title: <private> _idField :string
documents_model.html#_idField.name: _idField
documents_model.html#_idField.description: The name of the field that uniquely identifies a record. When provided, some operations will take advantage of it

documents_model.html#_pkey.title: <private> _pkey :*
documents_model.html#_pkey.name: _pkey
documents_model.html#_pkey.description: The value of the primary key if documents/model#_idField is filled in. It will be null if none found

documents_model.html#isNew.title: isNew :boolean
documents_model.html#isNew.name: isNew
documents_model.html#isNew.description: If documents/model#_idField is filled in and it's value is empty this will be true.

documents_model.html#isEmpty.title: isEmpty :boolean
documents_model.html#isEmpty.name: isEmpty
documents_model.html#isEmpty.description: Returns true if this instance is empty

documents_model.html#.update.title: <static> update( obj, qu, setDocument )
documents_model.html#.update.name: update
documents_model.html#.update.description: Updates all records in obj that match the query. See module:documents/probe.updateOperators for the operators that are supported.

documents_model.html#.find.title: <static> find( obj, qu ) → {array}
documents_model.html#.find.name: find
documents_model.html#.find.description: Find all records that match a query

documents_model.html#.findKeys.title: <static> findKeys( obj, qu ) → {array}
documents_model.html#.findKeys.name: findKeys
documents_model.html#.findKeys.description: Find all records that match a query and returns the keys for those items. This is similar to module:documents/probe.find but instead of returning records, returns the keys. If obj is an object it will return the hash key. If 'obj' is an array, it will return the index

documents_model.html#.findOne.title: <static> findOne( obj, qu ) → {object}
documents_model.html#.findOne.name: findOne
documents_model.html#.findOne.description: Returns the first record that matches the query. Aliased as seek.

documents_model.html#.findOneKey.title: <static> findOneKey( obj, qu ) → {object}
documents_model.html#.findOneKey.name: findOneKey
documents_model.html#.findOneKey.description: Returns the first record that matches the query and returns its key or index depending on whether obj is an object or array respectively. Aliased as seekKey.

documents_model.html#.remove.title: <static> remove( obj, qu ) → {object|array}
documents_model.html#.remove.name: remove
documents_model.html#.remove.description: Remove all items in the object/array that match the query

documents_model.html#.all.title: <static> all( obj, qu ) → {boolean}
documents_model.html#.all.name: all
documents_model.html#.all.description: Returns true if all items match the query

documents_model.html#.any.title: <static> any( obj, qu ) → {boolean}
documents_model.html#.any.name: any
documents_model.html#.any.description: Returns true if any of the items match the query

documents_model.html#.set.title: <static> set( path, record, setter, newValue )
documents_model.html#.set.name: set
documents_model.html#.set.description: This will write the value into a record at the path, creating intervening objects if they don't exist. This does not work as filtered update and is meant to be used on a single record. It is a nice way of setting a property at an arbitrary depth at will.

documents_model.html#.get.title: <static> get( path, record ) → {*}
documents_model.html#.get.name: get
documents_model.html#.get.description: Reaches into an object and allows you to get at a value deeply nested in an object. This is not a query, but a straight reach in, useful for event bindings

documents_model.html#.some.title: <static> some( obj, qu ) → {boolean}
documents_model.html#.some.name: some
documents_model.html#.some.description: Returns true if any of the items match the query. Aliases as any

documents_model.html#.every.title: <static> every( obj, qu ) → {boolean}
documents_model.html#.every.name: every
documents_model.html#.every.description: Returns true if all items match the query. Aliases as all

module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html.title: mixins/signalable~Signal
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html.name: Signal
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html.description: A signal that can be raised on an object. When you deploy the Signalable mixin, it creates instances of these for you.

module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#on.title: on( listener, listenerContext, priority ) → {SignalBinding}
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#on.name: on
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#on.description: Ties a listener to a signal.

module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#onc.title: once( listener, listenerContext, priority ) → {SignalBinding}
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#onc.name: once
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#onc.description: Ties a listener to for a signal for one execution.

module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#off.title: off( listener, listenerContext ) → {function}
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#off.name: off
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#off.description: Unbinds a listener to a signal.

module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#has.title: has( listener, listenerContext ) → {boolean}
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#has.name: has
module-mixins_signalable-Signal.html#has.description: Check if listener was attached to Signal.

module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#any.description: Returns true if any of the items match the query. Aliases as all

module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#rem.title: remove( qu ) → {object|array}
module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#rem.name: remove

module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#fin.title: findOneKey( qu ) → {object}

module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#fin.name: findOne
module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#fin.description: Returns the first record that matches the query. Aliased as seek.

This is similar to module:ink/probe.find but instead of returning records, returns the keys. If obj is an object it will return the hash key. If 'obj' is an array, it will return the index

module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#fin.title: find( qu ) → {array}
module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#fin.name: find
module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#fin.description: Find all records that match a query

module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#upd.title: update( qu, setDocument )
module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#upd.name: update
module-ink_collector-OCollector.html#upd.description: Updates all records in obj that match the query. See module:ink/probe.updateOperators for the operators that are supported.

module-ink_collector-ACollector.html#eve.description: Returns true if all items match the query. Aliases as all

module-ink_collector-ACollector.html#upd.description: Updates all records in obj that match the query. See module:ink/probe.updateOperators for the operators that are supported.

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: documents/probe.queryOperators
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: queryOperators
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: This is not actually a class, but an artifact of the documentation system

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $eq
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $eq
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $eq performs a === comparison by comparing the value directly if it is an atomic value. otherwise if it is an array, it checks to see if the value looked for is in the array. {field: value} or {field: {$eq : value}} or {array: value} or {array: {$eq : value}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $ne
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $ne
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $ne performs a !== comparison by comparing the value directly if it is an atomic value. Otherwise, if it is an array this is performs a "not in array". '{field: {$ne : value}} or '{array: {$ne : value}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $all
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $all
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $all checks to see if all of the members of the query are included in an array {array: {$all: [val1, val2, val3]}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $gt
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $gt
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $gt Sees if a field is greater than the value {field: {$gt: value}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $gte
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $gte
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $gte Sees if a field is greater than or equal to the value {field: {$gte: value}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $lt
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $lt
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $lt Sees if a field is less than the value {field: {$lt: value}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $lte
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $lte
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $lte Sees if a field is less than or equal to the value {field: {$lte: value}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $in
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $in
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $in Sees if a field has one of the values in the query {field: {$in: [test1, test2, test3,...]}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $nin
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $nin
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $nin Sees if a field has none of the values in the query {field: {$nin: [test1, test2, test3,...]}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $exists
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $exists
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: $exists Sees if a field exists. {field: {$exists: true|false}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $mod
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $mod
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Checks equality to a modulus operation on a field {field: {$mod: [divisor, remainder]}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $size
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $size
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Compares the size of the field/array to the query. This can be used on arrays, strings and objects (where it will count keys) {'field|array: {$size: value}}`

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $regex
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $regex
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Performs a regular expression test againts the field {field: {$regex: re, $options: reOptions}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $elemMatch
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $elemMatch
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: This is like $all except that it works with an array of objects or value. It checks to see the array matches all of the conditions of the query {array: {$elemMatch: {path: value, path: {$operation: value2}}}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $and
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $and
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Returns true if all of the conditions of the query are met {$and: [query1, query2, query3]}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $or
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $or
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Returns true if any of the conditions of the query are met {$or: [query1, query2, query3]}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $nor
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $nor
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Returns true if none of the conditions of the query are met {$nor: [query1, query2, query3]}

module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.title: <static> $not
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.name: $not
module-documents_probe.queryOperators.ht.description: Logical NOT on the conditions of the query {$not: [query1, query2, query3]}

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: documents/probe.updateOperators
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: updateOperators
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: This is not actually a class, but an artifact of the documentation system

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: <static> $inc
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: $inc
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: Increments a field by the amount you specify. It takes the form { $inc: { field1: amount } }

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: <static> $dec
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: $dec
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: Decrements a field by the amount you specify. It takes the form { $dec: { field1: amount }

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: <static> $unset
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: $unset
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: Removes the field from the object. It takes the form { $unset: { field1: "" } }

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: <static> $pop
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: $pop
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: The $pop operator removes the first or last element of an array. Pass $pop a value of 1 to remove the last element in an array and a value of -1 to remove the first element of an array. This will only work on arrays. Syntax: { $pop: { field: 1 } } or { $pop: { field: -1 } }

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: <static> $push
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: $push
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: The $push operator appends a specified value to an array. It looks like this: { $push: { <field>: <value> } }

module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.title: <static> $pull
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.name: $pull
module-documents_probe.updateOperators.h.description: The $pull operator removes all instances of a value from an existing array. It looks like this: { $pull: { field: <query> } }

documents_schema.html.title: documents/schema
documents_schema.html.name: documents/schema
documents_schema.html.description: The validator mixin provides access to the features of the JSON validation system

mixins_bussable.html.name: mixins/bussable

mixins_signalable.html.title: mixins/signalable

module-base.html.title: base

module-base.html.description: This is base definition for all composed classes defined by the system

module-base_chains.html.title: base/chains
module-base_chains.html.name: base/chains
module-base_chains.html.description: The chains define the primary composition elements (functions) that determine the order of execution.

module-documents_binder.html.title: documents/binder
module-documents_binder.html.name: documents/binder
module-documents_binder.html.description: allows you to bind a change watcher that looks for get and set operations on an arbitrary property of an object at at any depth. This allows you to look for changes or intercept values asynchronously or otherwise.

module-documents_model.html.title: documents/model
module-documents_model.html.name: documents/model
module-documents_model.html.description: A model is the first level if usable data-bearing entity in the system. It does NOT include any verbs for saving or anything like that, it is a pure, in memory data container

module-documents_probe.html.title: documents/probe
module-documents_probe.html.name: documents/probe
module-documents_probe.html.description: Queries objects in memory using a mongo-like notation for reaching into objects and filtering for records

module-documents_schema.html.title: documents/schema
module-documents_schema.html.name: documents/schema
module-documents_schema.html.description: Enables a schema and validation feature set to your document or other object.

module-mixins_bussable.html.title: mixins/bussable
module-mixins_bussable.html.name: mixins/bussable
module-mixins_bussable.html.description: Provides easy access to the system bus and provides some helper methods for doing so

module-mixins_signalable.html.title: mixins/signalable
module-mixins_signalable.html.name: mixins/signalable
module-mixins_signalable.html.description: Add the ability to fire signals on your objects. Signals are events, but hard coded into the object and don't rely on strings like other events and eventables

module-utils_logger.html.title: utils/logger
module-utils_logger.html.name: utils/logger
module-utils_logger.html.description: The logging system for papyrus is based on http://pimterry.github.io/loglevel/ and slightly decorated
